question: An early publication from the Framingham Heart Study identified 51 incidences of coronary disease among 424 men with high serum cholesterol (245 mg per 100 mL and above) over a 6-year period. Among 454 men with lower cholesterol (less than 210 mg per 100 mL), there were 16 incident cases.
answer:
$$
 egin{aligned}
& \hat{p}_1= rac{51}{424}=0.1203 \
& \hat{p}_2= rac{16}{454}=0.0352 \
& \widehat{R R}= rac{0.1203}{0.0352}=3.41
\end{aligned}
$$


The $95 \%$ confidence interval for the $\widehat{R R}$ is 1.98 to 5.89 .
